description: Fix the dev-proxy SIGTERM E2E test that fails in Docker because the worker process becomes a zombie after the proxy exits. Docker containers without --init have no init process to reap zombies, so process.kill(pid, 0) succeeds on the zombie PID indefinitely. The prior polling fix (5s timeout) does not help because the issue is zombie reaping, not shutdown timing.

userStories.title: Fix dev-proxy SIGTERM test to handle zombie processes in Docker
userStories.description: As a platform contributor running E2E tests in Docker, I want the 'SIGTERM kills worker and proxy exits cleanly' test to pass so that the full E2E suite is green
acceptanceCriteria: The test at e2e/dev-proxy.e2e.ts passes in Docker containers (without --init flag)
The test still verifies that the proxy exits cleanly on SIGTERM
The test still verifies the HTTP port is no longer listening after shutdown
